json_type.description: CSRF bypasses for JSON endpoints using content-type manipulation

payloads.payload: Remove CSRF token parameter entirely from request
payloads.description: Test if server validates token presence or just its value

payloads.description: Empty CSRF token to test blank value acceptance

payloads.payload: Use CSRF token from another user's session
payloads.description: Test if tokens are tied to user identity or just valid format

payloads.payload: Duplicate CSRF cookie: set csrf=attacker_value cookie and send csrf=attacker_value in body
payloads.description: Double-submit cookie bypass with attacker-controlled value

payloads.payload: Use Referer: https://target.com.attacker.com to bypass Referer-based CSRF protection
payloads.description: Referer header spoofing to bypass referer-only CSRF defenses

payloads.payload: Omit Referer header entirely (Referrer-Policy: no-referrer in attacker page)
payloads.description: No Referer header may bypass Referer-only validation
